meta.document: Ohio DFR Pilot Program — State Program Template Analysis
sources: Ohio DFR RFA Questions and Responses (DFR-App-Questions-Tracker.pdf)

program_overview.name: Ohio Drone First Responder Pilot Program
program_overview.purpose: Evaluate how drones improve situational awareness, reduce emergency response times, and enhance first responder safety across law enforcement, fire, and EMS
program_overview.scope: Statewide — equip municipalities with advanced drone systems, FAA-approved BVLOS operations, comprehensive training

financial_structure.funding_type: Reimbursement-based — agencies pay upfront, state reimburses
financial_structure.notes: Agencies must have budget authority to front costs before reimbursement. Cannot use private DFR-as-a-service providers — direct hardware procurement required.

vetting_criteria: Capability to deliver quality product and services
vendor_requirements.flexibility_note: Selected applicants must choose from pre-approved vendor list — no flexibility to propose outside-list platforms
vendor_requirements.custom_equipment_policy: Ground-based sensing systems supporting DAA or BVLOS are NOT eligible under this program

operational_requirements.notes: Program enables FAA-approved BVLOS without rooftop visual observers. SkyfireAI manages FAA regulatory compliance for participating agencies.

replication_signals: Ohio is first statewide DFR program — other states watching
Structure provides template: state agency as program manager + FAA regulatory consultant + UTM partner + pre-approved vendor list
Reimbursement model solves budget authority problem for small agencies
Centralized vendor vetting reduces per-agency compliance burden
states_likely_to_replicate: Texas (large law enforcement market, active BVLOS programs)
California (multiple active DFR programs, UASI funding available)
Florida (Camp Blanding / military adjacency, growing DFR market)
Arizona (Scottsdale precedent, favorable climate for year-round ops)
template_value_for_other_states.forge_opportunity: Monitor state legislature and ODOT-equivalent agencies for DFR pilot program bills. Each new state program = large procurement signal and report opportunity.

report_types: State DFR Program Tracker — which states have active programs, pending legislation, or planning stages
Vendor Pre-approval Intelligence — which vendors are on state approved lists
Agency Application Guide — step-by-step for agencies applying to state DFR programs
